results.title: What program or app can I use for digital backdrops?
results.content: Photoshop, Canva, and Evoto are popular options for digital backdrops, with some commenters also recommending LuminarNeo and PhotoRoom. Erin

results.title: 15 DIY Photo Backdrop Ideas for Small Spaces
results.content:
## 3. V-Flats for Professional Versatility

  

V-Flats are a go-to solution for photographers needing a portable, multi-use setup. These foldable foam boards can serve as solid white or black photography backdrops, helping to control light and reduce reflections. For a more artistic look, you can use colored lights and gels to create split light portraits or add a funky vibe.

Using the white side of a V-Flat as a backdrop to create the look of sunshine indoors.

Model standing in front of a white V-Flat as a backdrop.

Pro Tip: Use V-Flats not only as photography backdrops but also as light modifiers to bounce or block light. This dual-purpose setup is especially valuable in small spaces where it really helps if every tool can do double duty. [...] ## 10. Mirrored Surfaces for Artistic Reflection

  

Mirrored panels or tiles can double as photography backdrops and props. Lean a mirror against a wall or place smaller tiles together to reflect light creatively. Mirrors are great for abstract compositions or unique portraits, adding depth and interest to your images.

Woman model posing in studio with a mirror behind her.

Photo by Godisable Jacob

Pro Tip: Combine mirrors with other backdrops to create layered reflections. This adds complexity and intrigue to your shots.

  

## 11. DIY Greenery Walls for Natural Vibes [...] ## 4. Wallpaper Panels for a Customized Look

  

Peel-and-stick wallpaper panels are an excellent option for creating customizable backdrops. Textured designs like brick, wood, or floral patterns bring character to portraits or still-life photography.

Adhesive wall panels that look like white brick.

Pro Tip: Attach them to foam boards or large cardboard sheets to make them portable.

  

## 5. Craft Paper Rolls for Clean Simplicity

  

Large rolls of craft paper provide a smooth, minimalist backdrop that’s perfect for both portraits and product shots. Secure the roll to a rod, stand, or even tape it directly to a wall. Pull the paper down to your desired length, and cut it when you’re done for a fresh start next time.

Product photographer using colored paper as a backdrop.
